summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including zoning text amendments, police department reaccreditation, property insurance renewal, and the future of tornado-damaged Station 18 firehouse. The board voted unanimously to demolish Station 18 and build a new firehouse, citing the minimal cost difference between renovation and new construction and the importance of firefighter health and safety. The board also approved insurance renewal, records retention updates, personnel changes, and discussed the township gateway sign relocation.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (9/13/23 through 9/26/23) – passed unanimously
Motion to renew property insurance with Highland and Ohio Plan at cost not to exceed $122,185 – passed unanimously
Motion to immediately demolish Station 18 and begin path to build a new firehouse – passed unanimously
Motion to accept donation of eight hand vacuums from Bryan Equipment – passed (Allen yes, Hodges abstain, Hausermann yes)
Motion to dispose of records in accordance with existing RC2 with Historical Society given opportunity to retain items – passed unanimously
Motion to approve revised RC2 records retention schedule – passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of service worker Dale Garrett effective 9/27/23 – passed unanimously
Motion to accept Ohio Ambulance Transportation Program ARPA grant of $20,227.76 – passed unanimously
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Jen Farmer to O2X workshop October 18-19 at no cost – passed unanimously
Motion to enter executive session at 7:34 PM – passed unanimously
Motion to promote Charlie Thomas from part-time to full-time service worker effective 10/2/23 at $21.15 with one-year probationary period – passed unanimously
Motion to hire Melinda Eichenhorst as HR manager and assistant to the administrator effective 9/27/23 at $62,400 annual salary – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 8:58 PM – passed unanimously

Zoning consultant to forward camper/temporary living language to Administrator Rose for zoning amendment process
Zoning consultant to group remaining zoning topics (tiny homes, accessory dwelling units, farm animals on small lots, northeast quadrant 2-acre minimum) for future workshops
Laura (project specialist) to confirm gateway sign changes with Justin at One Stop: 45-degree angle, hillside placement, 6-foot height, blue background with white township lettering
Administrator Rose to coordinate with new ODOT District 9 contact regarding sidewalk and road concerns on Goshen Road
Administrator Rose to work with Carrie on insurance adjustments if/when Station 18 decisions are finalized
Staff to begin sorting basement records with Goshen Historical Society present to identify items of historical value
Administrator Rose to bring list of surplus items for future donation/GovDeals consideration
Don and department heads to plan trunk-or-treat event for October 31st in administration parking lot area
Chief Myers to proceed with Station 18 demolition and new firehouse planning
Administrator Rose to continue working with state legislators (Rep. Jean Schmidt, Sen. Johnson) on $2.5 million funding bill

$114,094 – insurance premium if Station 18 removed from policy
$1,090,413.14 – final insurance payout for tornado damage
$977,631.71 – township out-of-pocket cost if rebuilding Station 18
$85,946.71 – cost difference between new construction and repair of Station 18
$130/sq ft – average soft cost for new firehouse construction

$20,227.76 – Ohio Ambulance Transportation Program ARPA grant accepted
$62,400 – annual salary for new HR manager Melinda Eichenhorst

Police department CALEA reaccreditation (one of only 7 small agencies in Ohio)
Station 18 firehouse demolition and new construction decision following tornado damage
Property insurance renewal with Ohio Plan/Highland
Sidewalk safety concerns along Goshen Road and coordination with county engineer/ODOT
New digital gateway sign relocation and design modifications
Records retention and basement records disposal with Historical Society involvement
Subdivision development concerns from residents regarding growth and school capacity
Homerama coming to Goshen Township on Klosterman Farm (Smith Road)
Township trunk-or-treat event planning for October 31st
Personnel actions: resignation, promotion, and new HR manager hire
Detectives recognized by Cincinnati Crimestoppers for crime-solving work
attendees: Marsha Collier (public speaker, candidate for recorder)
notes: The meeting was emotionally charged during the Station 18 discussion. Administrator Rose shared news of a Northern Kentucky firefighter dying from service-related cancer, connecting it to the importance of building a modern firehouse with proper health protections. The board entered executive session at 7:34 PM and returned at 8:56 PM. Trustee Hodges abstained from the Bryan Equipment vacuum donation vote. The Pumpkin Run event was announced at Clermont County Fairgrounds. Representative Jean Schmidt and Senator Johnson are working on a $2.5 million state funding bill for the firehouse. The OEMA 75% grant match has a 4-5 year maximum timeline. The transcript contained a long series of repeated 'Bye' words likely from audio artifacts.
title: BOT 9-26-23
